failure_surface: Code execution failed with SyntaxError due to malformed code block formatting - special characters ("<tool_call>") were incorrectly included at the start of Python code, preventing any spreadsheet manipulation from occurring.
root_cause: The agent repeatedly submitted code blocks with incorrect formatting syntax (special markers/characters) that the Python interpreter could not parse, causing all execution attempts to fail before any data inspection or formula creation could take place.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Ensure code blocks contain only valid Python syntax without extraneous formatting markers or special characters when submitting to the code interpreter tool.
evidence: Multiple identical SyntaxError occurrences pointing to line 1 of each code block

All six conversation attempts show "Cell In[1], line 1
     <tool_call>
    ^
SyntaxError: invalid syntax"
No data loading, inspection, or formula creation was attempted due to parsing failure
Same malformed code repeated across six attempts without format correction

memory_items.title: Clean Code Block Formatting
memory_items.lesson: Code blocks submitted to interpreters must contain only valid language syntax without special formatting markers or non-executable characters.
memory_items.generalization: Always validate that the raw content sent to an execution environment contains no extraneous whitespace, formatting tags, or special characters before submission.

memory_items.title: Execution Failure Response Pattern
memory_items.lesson: When code fails to execute due to syntax errors, do not repeat the same submission without correcting the underlying formatting issue.
memory_items.generalization: Upon receiving a syntax error, immediately review the code block structure and formatting before resubmitting - identical failures indicate uncorrected root causes.

memory_items.title: Tool Invocation Hygiene
memory_items.lesson: Ensure proper separation between tool invocation markers and executable code content when using code interpreter interfaces.
memory_items.generalization: Verify the complete message payload reaches the execution engine in a parsable format - inspect how the system wraps and delivers code to avoid delimiter/format collisions.

edits.content:
## Execution Environment Best Practices

### Code Submission Integrity
- Ensure code blocks contain **only valid language syntax** when submitting to interpreters - no special formatting markers, hidden characters, or extra whitespace at line starts.
- When encountering repeated `SyntaxError` failures, **do not resubmit identical code**. The error indicates formatting corruption rather than logic problems.
- Validate that the raw payload reaching the execution environment is clean and parsable before assuming computational issues exist.

### Error Recovery Discipline
- Treat syntax errors on line 1 as **tool/interface issues**, not algorithmic bugs - check how the execution wrapper processes your code.
- Upon repeated identical failures, pause to audit the message structure rather than blindly retrying.
- Maintain clear boundaries between tool invocation protocols and executable code content.
